title: phlox speciosa subsp. lanceolata
intro: Phlox speciosa subsp. lanceolata, also known as the western phlox, is a perennial flowering plant native to the western regions of North America. The plant's root system has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a valuable remedy for wound healing, sore throats, and digestive issues. Western phlox is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and treating menstrual disorders due to its estrogenic properties. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
